title: CLI agent commands: pp agent spawn/dismiss/status/list/prune
description: Implement CLI subcommands: 'pp agent spawn --role imp --repo <name> [--task <id>]' (calls daemon agent.spawn), 'pp agent dismiss <name> --repo <name>' (calls agent.dismiss), 'pp agent status [name]' (shows agent status with liveness check), 'pp agent list [--repo <name>]' (lists agents), 'pp agent prune [--branch-age 24h]' (cleanup dead/stopped agents). All commands talk to daemon via IPC, never call tmux/git directly. Must include tests for CLI argument parsing and command dispatch. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments and usage help text.

title: Workflow types, CUE schema, and loader
description: Create internal/workflow/ package with: (1) Core types: Workflow, Step (union), Param, Aspect, AspectMatch, Instance, InstanceStatus, StepResult, WorkflowContext, StepHandler interface. (2) Embedded CUE schema (schema.cue via go:embed) defining #Workflow, #Step, #SpawnStep, #WaitStep, #EvaluateStep, #DismissStep, #GateStep, #Aspect, #AspectMatch, #Param. (3) Loader with two-phase parse/resolve: parseWorkflowFromFile (stubs _input/_ctx, validates structure, extracts params) and ResolveWorkflow (injects concrete params, validates with cue.Concrete(true), JSON marshal to Go structs). (4) CUE module infrastructure: EnsureModuleInfrastructure() creating cue.mod/module.cue, tasks/tasks.cue, aspects/aspects.cue. (5) Aspect expansion: expandAspects() pure function with AND-match semantics, filepath.Match for name globs, single-pass per-aspect. (6) Context variable resolution: ResolveStepConfig() re-parsing CUE with current _ctx values. Tests: schema validation, two-phase loading, param resolution, aspect expansion, context injection.

title: Output formatting: table/json/text with TTY auto-detect
description: Create internal/output/ package with three formatters: table.go (fixed-width columns, colored status via isatty, relative durations), json.go (compact JSON, snake_case fields, ISO 8601 timestamps), text.go (space-separated, one line per record). Implement TTY auto-detection: table when stdout is terminal, JSON when piped. Support --output flag override (table, json, text, json-pretty). Errors go to stderr in matching format. Notification output to stderr with [notification] prefix. Must include tests for each formatter and TTY detection. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 2 Epic: Daemon Architecture
description: Implement the long-running Maggie VM daemon that hosts the tuplespace, serves CLI commands over JSON-RPC/Unix socket, manages image checkpointing and crash recovery, and handles graceful shutdown. The daemon is the kernel of procyon-park — everything else talks to it.

title: Feedback loop + cross-pollination
description: Create internal/bbs/feedback.go and crosspoll.go. Feedback loop runs on configurable interval (default 24h), writes warm-tier analytics back to hot tuplespace as furniture. (1) Convention pruning: query ConventionEffectiveness, delete conventions that hurt outcomes, write fact for those that helped. (2) Obstacle surfacing: query ObstacleClusters, write obstacle_cluster facts. (3) Repo health: query AgentPerformance, write repo_health facts per scope. (4) Workflow signature caching: query WorkflowSignatures, cache failed signatures as furniture for GC's workflow warnings. (5) Knowledge flow surfacing: query KnowledgeFlow, write knowledge_flow facts. (6) FeedbackResult tracking: counts for each action + errors. Cross-pollination (crosspoll.go): detect when tuple payload references another scope (substring match), write notification tuples for referenced scope agents. Rate limit: 1 per (source_agent, target_scope) per 5min. Tests: each feedback action, convention pruning logic, cross-pollination rate limiting.

title: Prime middleware pipeline + user overrides
description: Implement the prime middleware pipeline in internal/prime/pipeline.go: loadTemplate → applyUserOverride → injectAddendum → injectContext → render. Each step independently testable. User-customizable overrides: check ~/.procyon-park/instructions/<role>.txt, if exists use instead of embedded template. Implement ExportTemplates() that copies all embedded templates to instructions dir for editing. Custom templates take priority but still get BBS addendum and context appended. Implement context budget: track approximate token count, trim/summarize when exceeding configurable limit (default 4000 tokens). Must include tests for full pipeline, user override precedence, export, and context budget trimming. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 5 integration tests: priming end-to-end
description: Write integration tests exercising the full priming system: render each role template and verify structure (identity, workflow, completion protocol sections present), test user override precedence (custom file overrides embedded), test BBS addendum contains all required sections (claiming protocol, CLI reference), test tuplespace context injection (seed tuples, verify they appear in context), test context budget trimming (large tuplespace doesn't blow up context), test pp prime CLI round-trip through daemon, test ExportTemplates writes all roles. All existing tests must continue to pass alongside new tests.

title: Phase 4 Research: CLI Design
description: Research the CLI architecture. Cover: thin Go binary vs Maggie script vs single binary with subcommands, IPC protocol for CLI-to-daemon communication, command structure (spawn/dismiss/list/bbs/mail/workflow/telemetry etc), agent-facing commands (what agents type in tmux), argument parsing, output formatting. Should the CLI be a Go cobra app like current imp, or something else? Reference imp-castle's internal/cli/ (39 commands). Output: docs/research/phase-4-cli.md
close_reason: Research complete: docs/research/phase-4-cli.md covers CLI architecture, IPC protocol, command structure, output formatting, and comparison with imp-castle

title: Phase 2 integration tests: daemon lifecycle end-to-end
description: Write integration tests that exercise the full daemon lifecycle: start daemon, connect via Unix socket, perform JSON-RPC BBS operations (write/read/take/scan tuples), verify image checkpoint on disk, graceful shutdown, and restart with state recovery from checkpoint. Tests must verify the complete round-trip from CLI through IPC to VM and back. All existing tests (smoke + Phase 1 BBS) must continue to pass alongside new tests.

title: GC engine + escalation detection
description: Create internal/bbs/gc.go and escalation.go. GC loop runs every 60s with passes: (1) collectExpiredEphemeral: delete tuples past TTL, archive first, log unclaimed expired. (2) cleanupStaleClaims: stale (1h + task_done) and abandoned (2h, no task_done) claims. Pre-scan task_done events for doneTasks set. (3) promoteConventions: group convention-proposals by canonical content, promote at quorum (2+ distinct agents), write furniture convention to global scope. (4) archiveCompletedTasks: for each task_done, run synthesis then archive by task_id and agent_id. Skip agents with pending dismiss_request. Escalation detection (every GC cycle): (5) detectSystemicObstacles: 2+ obstacles same scope → systemic_obstacle event + king notification. (6) detectUnclaimedNeeds: needs >10min with no agent_id → king escalation (once per need). (7) detectRepeatedFailures: 3+ task_failed same scope → repo_health_warning furniture fact. (8) detectWorkflowWarnings: compare active tasks against failed workflow signatures, 60%+ match → early_warning. Tests: each GC pass, escalation triggers, archive-before-delete, event exclusion.

title: Image checkpointing: atomic writes, periodic timer, crash recovery
description: Implement atomic image checkpointing with the .tmp/.prev rotation pattern (write .tmp → fsync → rename .prev → rename .tmp → fsync dir). Add periodic checkpoint timer (configurable interval, default 5min). Implement crash recovery on startup: detect .tmp/.prev files and restore from best available. Must include tests for atomic write correctness, crash recovery scenarios (interrupted at each stage), and timer-triggered checkpoints. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments on all exported types and functions.

title: Cobra root command + IPC client + daemon auto-start
description: Set up Cobra framework: create internal/cli/root.go with root command, global flags (--socket, --output, --quiet, --verbose, --no-color), and Execute(). Create internal/ipc/client.go with Call(socketPath, method, params) JSON-RPC client (~30 lines) and types.go with Request/Response structs. Implement daemon auto-start in ensureDaemon(): try connect, if fail start daemon in background, wait up to 5s for socket. Implement semantic exit codes (0=success, 1=error, 2=usage, 3=connection, 4=timeout, 5=not-found, 130=interrupted). Update cmd/pp/main.go to use cli.Execute(). Must include tests for IPC client, auto-start logic, and exit code mapping. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 4 Epic: CLI Design
description: Implement the full CLI using Cobra: migrate existing daemon/agent commands to Cobra framework under internal/cli/, add IPC client package, output formatters (table/json/text with TTY auto-detect), BBS commands, repo/config/workflow command groups, convenience commands (prime, status, version, init, completion, ping), daemon auto-start, and semantic exit codes (0-5, 130). Binary name: pp.

title: Cross-phase contradiction analysis of research documents
description:
Review all 10 research documents in docs/research/ (phase-0 through phase-9) and identify any contradictions, inconsistencies, or conflicting assumptions between phases. For each contradiction found, document:
1. Which phases conflict
2. The specific contradiction (quote both sides)
3. A recommended resolution

Areas to watch for:
- IPC protocol choices (JSON-RPC vs gRPC Connect vs other) - do all phases agree?
- Storage layer assumptions (SQLite vs BadgerDB vs image persistence) - who stores what?
- Agent model (Maggie objects vs Go structs) - consistent across phases?
- CLI architecture (Go binary vs Maggie script) - one answer?
- Config format and paths - consistent naming?
- Tuplespace schema and categories - same vocabulary everywhere?
- Mail-as-tuple vs separate mail system
- Daemon responsibilities - any overlap/gaps between phases?
- Go interop primitives - does phase 0 cover everything later phases need?
- Workflow engine - CUE loading assumptions consistent with phase 0 interop?

Output: docs/research/cross-phase-contradiction-analysis.md
close_reason: Analysis complete: 12 contradictions identified across 10 research phases (4 hard, 5 soft, 3 minor)

title: Warm tier: Parquet archive via DuckDB
description: Create internal/bbs/archive.go for warm tier storage. (1) Parquet export: in-memory DuckDB creates temp export table matching tuple schema, inserts tuples, COPY TO Parquet. Directory layout: ~/.procyon-park/bbs/warm/<YYYY-MM>/<scope>/<groupKey>.parquet. (2) Archive-then-delete pattern: Parquet written before SQLite delete commits — crash-safe (worst case duplicate data, never loss). (3) parquetGlob() helper builds scope-filtered or wildcard glob expressions for DuckDB read_parquet(). (4) openDuckDB() helper for ephemeral in-memory DuckDB connections (analytics query engine pattern). (5) ArchiveByTaskID and ArchiveByAgentID for session tuple archival. (6) Event exclusion: task_done and dismiss_request events excluded from archival until king acts. Tests: export/read roundtrip, glob building, archive-then-delete atomicity, event exclusion.

title: [epic] Phase 6: Workflow Engine
description: CUE-based workflow engine for automated multi-step agent orchestration. Covers: CUE schema + loader with two-phase parse/resolve, state machine executor, step handlers (spawn/wait/evaluate/dismiss/gate), aspect expansion, crash recovery, approval gates, CLI commands, and integration tests.

title: Analytics queries: 6 DuckDB warm-tier queries
description: Create internal/bbs/analytics.go with 6 analytical queries over warm-tier Parquet files. All follow pattern: open fresh in-memory DuckDB, build read_parquet(glob), execute SQL, return typed Go structs. (1) QueryAgentPerformance: group by scope, count obstacles/artifacts/agents, compute artifact-to-obstacle ratio. (2) QueryTimeToFirstObstacle: avg seconds between task start and first obstacle per scope. (3) QueryConventionEffectiveness: before/after success rates around convention introduction date (minTasks=3). (4) QueryObstacleClusters: group by payload description, return clusters with minOccurrences(3)+, distinct agents, first/last seen. (5) QueryWorkflowSignatures: dominant tuple emission pattern for success vs failed tasks using ROW_NUMBER window functions. (6) QueryKnowledgeFlow: trace cross-agent knowledge propagation — tuples by one agent present when others completed tasks. Tests: each query with known Parquet test data, edge cases (empty data, single task).

title: LLM synthesis: knowledge extraction before archival
description: Create internal/bbs/synthesis.go for LLM-powered knowledge extraction. (1) SynthesisConfig: Enabled toggle, Provider (anthropic/openai), Model (default claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929), APIKey from config or env. (2) Prompt design: present full tuple history as [category/scope] identity (agent=X, lifecycle=Y) -- payload, ask LLM for JSON array of extracted knowledge tuples with category, scope, identity, payload.content. (3) callLLMStandalone dispatcher to callAnthropicStandalone or callOpenAIStandalone (90s timeout). (4) Response parsing with markdown code fence handling. (5) Write extracted tuples as furniture with instance=synthesized, using Upsert for idempotency. (6) Best-effort: synthesis errors logged but never block archival. Tests: prompt building, response parsing, provider dispatch, error handling, Upsert idempotency.

title: Phase 8: Telemetry & OTEL Integration
description: Two-tier telemetry system: SQLite event store for SDLC activity events + DuckDB/Parquet columnar analytics for OTEL signal ingestion. Embedded OTLP receiver (gRPC+HTTP), unified query engine, and Maggie VM OTEL primitives.
close_reason: Phase 8 complete: 19 files, 7541 lines. SQLite event store, OTLP receiver, DuckDB pipeline, Parquet export, query engine, Maggie primitives, telemetry CLI, integration tests.

title: SQLite telemetry event store
description: Port TelemetryStore from imp-castle. SQLite-backed event store with TelemetryEvent struct (ID, Timestamp, EventType, Category, Source, Target, Data, NodeID, SyncedAt). Key structure: telemetry/{RFC3339Nano}/{eventId}. 7 event categories: agent, task, mail, git, session, error, workflow. 30-day retention with cleanup. Store/retrieve/list/purge operations. Tests: CRUD, time-range queries, retention cleanup, concurrent access.
close_reason: SQLite telemetry event store implemented with full test coverage. All tests passing.

title: OTLP receiver: gRPC + HTTP ingestion
description: Embedded OTLP receiver accepting traces, metrics, and logs. Dual transport: gRPC on :4317, HTTP on :4318. Localhost-only binding. Buffered output channels (256 capacity). Non-blocking writes with drop on full (backpressure via drop). HTTP endpoints: POST /v1/traces, /v1/metrics, /v1/logs (application/x-protobuf, 4MiB body limit). GracefulStop for gRPC, Shutdown for HTTP. Dependencies: go.opentelemetry.io/proto/otlp/collector/{traces,metrics,logs}/v1, google.golang.org/grpc. Tests: receiver start/stop, gRPC+HTTP ingestion, backpressure behavior, graceful shutdown.
close_reason: OTLP receiver with gRPC+HTTP ingestion, backpressure, graceful shutdown. 12 tests passing.

title: DuckDB pipeline + schema
description: In-memory DuckDB pipeline consuming from OTLP receiver channels. Three tables: otel_spans (trace/span IDs, name, kind, timing, duration_ns, status, agent_name, task_id, repo_name, LLM fields: model_name, tokens, cost), otel_metrics (gauge/sum/histogram with type-specific nullable columns), otel_logs (severity, trace correlation, body). Protobuf-to-schema conversion extracting imp correlation attributes (imp.agent.name, imp.task.id, imp.repository.name, gen_ai.request.model). consumeLoop() reads channels and inserts. Dependencies: github.com/marcboeker/go-duckdb. Tests: schema creation, protobuf conversion, consume loop, DuckDB queries on inserted data.
close_reason: DuckDB pipeline with schema, protobuf conversion, consume loop. All tests passing.

title: Parquet export + flush loop
description: Periodic flush from in-memory DuckDB to Parquet files. flushLoop() every 5 minutes (configurable DefaultFlushInterval). Export via COPY TO FORMAT PARQUET COMPRESSION ZSTD. Time-partitioned: {warmDir}/{YYYY-MM}/{signal}.parquet (spans.parquet, metrics.parquet, logs.parquet). Export-then-purge strategy (crash-safe: duplicates > data loss). Final flush on Stop(). Tests: flush creates correct Parquet files, partitioning by month, ZSTD compression, crash-recovery semantics, final flush on shutdown.
close_reason: Parquet export with flush loop, ZSTD compression, monthly partitioning, export-then-purge safety. 8 tests passing.

title: Unified query engine
description: QueryEngine providing unified view over in-memory DuckDB + warm-tier Parquet files. unionQuery() builds CTE combining both sources. Operations: LookupTrace(id), SearchSpans(params) with filters (name, agent, task, duration, time), AggregateMetrics(params) with SUM/AVG/MAX/MIN/COUNT + GROUP BY, SearchLogs(params) with filters (body, severity, trace, agent). IPC integration: HandleQuery() dispatches JSON command requests for CLI access. NewStandaloneQueryEngine() for read-only Parquet queries without running pipeline. Tests: unified queries, each operation, IPC dispatch, standalone mode.
close_reason: Unified query engine with LookupTrace, SearchSpans, AggregateMetrics, SearchLogs, IPC HandleQuery, standalone mode. All tests passing.

title: Maggie VM OTEL primitives
description: Add OTEL trace/metric/log primitives to Maggie's VM via Go FFI. Add tracer field to VM (go.opentelemetry.io/otel/trace). Configure OTEL exporter to send to localhost:4317. Register primitives: Telemetry span:do: (creates traced span), Telemetry setAttribute:value:, Telemetry counter:increment:attributes:, Telemetry log:severity:attributes:. Propagate context.Context through Maggie's call stack / block evaluation. Resource attributes: service.name=maggie, imp.agent.name, imp.repository.name, imp.task.id, maggie.process.name. Tests: span creation, attribute setting, metric emission, log emission, context propagation.
close_reason: Maggie VM OTEL primitives: span creation, log emission, context propagation, daemon wiring. Tests passing.

title: Telemetry CLI commands + daemon bridge
description: CLI commands for telemetry interaction. (1) 'pp telemetry status' — show receiver status, pipeline stats, last flush time. (2) 'pp telemetry search spans [--agent] [--task] [--name] [--min-duration]' — search spans. (3) 'pp telemetry search logs [--severity] [--agent] [--body]' — search logs. (4) 'pp telemetry trace <trace-id>' — lookup full trace. (5) 'pp telemetry metrics [--name] [--agg]' — aggregate metrics. (6) 'pp telemetry export [--format parquet|json] [--output]' — export data. Daemon bridge: register telemetry.* RPC methods. Output: text tables + JSON. Tests: CLI arg parsing, output formatting.
close_reason: Telemetry CLI commands (status, search spans/logs, trace lookup, metrics, export) + daemon bridge. All tests passing.

title: Phase 8 integration tests: telemetry end-to-end
description: Comprehensive integration tests for the full telemetry pipeline. (1) SDLC event roundtrip: emit events via store, query back, verify. (2) OTLP ingestion roundtrip: send spans/metrics/logs via gRPC, verify in DuckDB. (3) Flush-and-query: ingest data, trigger flush, verify Parquet files, query via unified engine. (4) Maggie integration: Maggie process emits span, verify it arrives in pipeline. (5) CLI roundtrip: ingest data, query via CLI commands, verify output. (6) Retention: verify event cleanup after TTL. (7) Crash recovery: simulate crash mid-flush, verify no data loss. (8) Standalone query: verify Parquet-only queries without running pipeline.
close_reason: All 8 integration tests passing: SDLC roundtrip, OTLP ingestion, flush+query, Maggie primitives, CLI roundtrip, retention, crash recovery, standalone query.

title: Phase 3 Research: Agent Lifecycle & Identity
description: Research agent lifecycle management in Maggie. Cover: Agent as first-class Maggie object (identity, role, state, mailbox), spawn/dismiss/respawn flows, tmux session creation and management via Go primitives, git worktree creation/cleanup, agent registry persistence, name generation, environment variable injection, agent liveness detection, crash recovery with state preservation. Reference imp-castle's internal/agent/, internal/tmux/, internal/git/, internal/state/, internal/impname/. Output: docs/research/phase-3-agent-lifecycle.md

title: Smoke test: verify all 8 Maggie primitives work from procyon-park
description:
Create smoke tests that verify each of the 8 native Maggie primitives works in the procyon-park build context:
1. ExternalProcess: run 'echo hello' and verify stdout
2. UnixSocketServer/Client: listen, connect, send/receive, close
3. Json: encode a Dictionary, decode it back, verify round-trip
4. SqliteDatabase: open in-memory, create table, insert, query, close
5. Toml: decode a TOML string, verify values
6. DuckDatabase: open in-memory, query, close
7. CueContext: load a CUE string, validate, extract value
8. VM saveImageAtomic: checkpoint and restore

Put tests in test/smoke_test.mag. Each test should be a method on a SmokeTest class that returns Success or raises on failure. Main.runSmokeTests should run all 8 and report results.

This validates that the procyon-park binary has all primitives correctly linked.
close_reason: All 8 smoke tests pass: ExternalProcess, UnixSocket, Json, Sqlite, Toml, DuckDB, CUE, and atomic image checkpoint

title: Phase 6 Research: Workflow Engine
description: Research the workflow engine design. Cover: CUE workflow definition loading and validation, state machine executor in Maggie, step types (spawn/wait/evaluate/dismiss/gate), aspects (cross-cutting step injection), workflow instance tracking, approval gates, timeout handling. Then explore what a Smalltalk-native workflow DSL might look like as a future alternative to CUE. Reference imp-castle's internal/workflow/. Output: docs/research/phase-6-workflows.md

title: Resolve cross-phase contradictions in research documents
description:
Update all research documents in docs/research/ to resolve the 12 contradictions identified in cross-phase-contradiction-analysis.md. Apply these resolutions:

HARD CONTRADICTIONS:
C1: Phase 6 - Replace all BadgerDB references with SQLite. Workflow state lives in SQLite, not Badger. Update executor struct, persistence descriptions, bucket/key patterns to SQL tables.
C2: Phase 6 - Redesign Wait and Gate steps to use tuplespace instead of mail. Wait polls for event tuples (task_done), Gate writes gate_request tuples and polls for gate_response tuples.
C3: Phase 3 - Clarify that agent lifecycle operations go through daemon IPC. The Go code in Phase 3 becomes daemon-side implementation behind the IPC interface. CLI never calls tmux/git directly.
C4: Phase 0 - Remove CUE from gowrap candidates. Add a section for hand-written CUE primitives explaining the deep integration needed (module loading, two-phase compilation, unification, context injection).

SOFT INCONSISTENCIES:
I1: Phase 0 - Standardize on modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go). Update dependency list and gowrap config.
I2: Phases 2, 4, 9 - Standardize all env vars on PP_ prefix.
I3: Phases 0, 4, 9 - Standardize binary name as pp. Fix Phase 0 Makefile output and Phase 9 command examples.
I4: Phase 3 - Change tmux session prefix from imp- to pp-.
I5: Phase 7 - Scope the 'no persistent DuckDB state' claim to analytics only, note Phase 8 uses DuckDB as in-memory buffer.

MINOR GAPS:
G1: Phase 1 - Fix convention promotion scan to use correct category.
G2: Phase 3 - Add Maggie adaptation section following patterns from Phases 1 and 2 (agent state in tuplespace, Go for system ops via primitives, Maggie for orchestration).
G3: Phase 6 - Note that _ctx is internal to workflow engine, step results also written to tuplespace for observability.

Be surgical - only change what's needed to resolve contradictions. Don't rewrite entire documents.
close_reason: All 12 contradictions resolved in research docs

title: IPC server: Unix socket listener + JSON-RPC transport
description: Implement the Unix socket listener at ~/.procyon-park/daemon.sock with JSON-RPC 2.0 transport. Handle connection lifecycle (accept, read, dispatch, respond, close). Support concurrent client connections with per-connection goroutines. Clean up stale socket files on startup. Must include tests for socket creation/cleanup, connection handling, and JSON-RPC request/response framing. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments on all exported types and functions.

title: Daemon core: Go struct, PID file, signals, VMWorker
description: Implement the daemon's core Go infrastructure: DaemonServer struct holding VM + TupleStore + config, PID file management (write on start, remove on stop, stale detection), OS signal handling (SIGTERM/SIGINT for graceful shutdown, double-signal escape hatch), and the VMWorker goroutine pattern that serializes all VM access through a channel. Must include unit tests for PID file lifecycle and signal handling. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments on all exported types and functions.

title: Workflow CLI commands and daemon bridge
description: Add workflow commands to internal/cli/ and daemon bridge. (1) CLI commands: 'pp workflow run <name> [--param key=value] [--repo]' — run a workflow, 'pp workflow list [--repo] [--status]' — list instances, 'pp workflow show <id> [--repo]' — show instance details with step results, 'pp workflow cancel <id>' — cancel running instance, 'pp workflow approve <id>' — approve human gate, 'pp workflow reject <id> --reason "..."' — reject human gate, 'pp workflow defs' — list available workflow definitions. (2) Daemon bridge in internal/daemon/workflowbridge.go: register workflow.* RPC methods (run, list, show, cancel, approve, reject, defs). Wire executor lifecycle to daemon start/stop. (3) Output formatting: text tables for list, detailed view for show, JSON for all. Tests: CLI arg parsing, daemon bridge dispatch, output formatting.

title: Imp name pool + agent registry via tuplespace
description: Implement imp name generation and agent registry using the BBS tuplespace (not JSON files). Name pool: 50 cutesy names, NextName(repoName) randomly selects from available and marks in-use via tuple, ReleaseName(repoName, name) returns to pool, fallback to numeric imp-N if pool exhausted. Agent registry: RegisterAgent(repoName, agent) writes agent tuple, UnregisterAgent removes it, GetAgent/ListAgents queries, UpdateAgentStatus updates status field. Agent struct fields: Name, Role, TmuxSession, Worktree, Branch, Task, EpicID, CreatedAt, Status (active/stopped/dead). Both Maggie classes and Go primitives as needed for tuplespace interaction. Must include tests for name allocation/release/exhaustion-fallback, registry CRUD, and concurrent access. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 4 integration tests: full CLI end-to-end
description: Write integration tests exercising the full CLI through Cobra: start daemon, run pp bbs out/in/rd/scan through CLI, verify output formatting (table/json/text modes), test agent spawn/dismiss/list via CLI, test daemon auto-start (stop daemon, run CLI command, verify daemon starts automatically), test exit codes (timeout=4, not-found=5, connection-error=3), test TTY detection behavior, test notification piggybacking on stderr. All existing tests (smoke + Phase 1-3) must continue to pass alongside new tests.

title: TupleGC: garbage collection process
description:
Implement periodic garbage collection for the tuplespace:

src/bbs/TupleGC.mag:
- TupleGC subclass of Object with instanceVars: space store interval running
- Factory: on:interval: (takes TupleSpace and Duration)
- start - forks a process that runs collection cycles
- stop - sets running to false
- Collection methods (each a separate method for clarity):
  - collectExpiredEphemeral: delete ephemeral tuples past TTL (archive first to DuckDB if available)
  - cleanupStaleClaims: claims >1h with task_done event or >2h unconditionally
  - promoteConventions: when 2+ agents propose same convention, promote to furniture
  - detectSystemicObstacles: log warning when 2+ obstacles in same scope
  - detectUnclaimedNeeds: log warning for needs unclaimed >10 minutes
- GC interval default: 60 seconds
- All SQL queries go through the Store (Go interop)
- Archival to DuckDB is optional/deferred - just delete for now with a hook point for later

title: TupleSpace: 4 Linda operations with blocking
description:
Implement the TupleSpace class with the four Linda operations:

src/bbs/TupleSpace.mag:
- TupleSpace subclass of Object with instanceVars: store waiters mutex
- Factory: open: dbPath (creates TupleStore, initializes mutex and waiters list)
- out: aTuple - insert into store, wake matching waiters, return tuple ID
- in: aPattern - destructive blocking read with furniture protection; default 5s timeout
- in: aPattern timeout: aDuration - with explicit timeout
- rd: aPattern - non-destructive blocking read; default 5s timeout
- rd: aPattern timeout: aDuration
- scan: aPattern - non-blocking match-all, returns Array
- store accessor for direct store access

src/bbs/Waiter.mag:
- Waiter subclass of Object with instanceVars: pattern destructive channel
- Factory: pattern:destructive: (creates unbuffered Channel)
- resume: aTuple - sends tuple on channel
- isDestructive accessor

Blocking semantics:
- waitFor:destructive:timeout: private method using Channel select with timeout channel
- Furniture protection: in() refuses to consume furniture tuples (returns Failure)
- Waiter wake-up in out() skips furniture for destructive waiters
- CancellationContext support: in:withContext: variant

title: Phase 0 Research: Project Setup & Go Interop Primitives
description: Research how to set up the procyon-park project structure, build system (single binary packaging), and critically: the Go interop primitives Maggie needs. Cover: SQLite bindings, DuckDB bindings, process exec (for git/tmux), Unix socket server/client, file I/O, TOML parsing, CUE evaluation. Survey Maggie's current primitive system and design the interop layer. Output: docs/research/phase-0-project-setup.md
close_reason: Research complete: docs/research/phase-0-project-setup.md covers project structure, build system, and all 7 Go interop primitives

title: Step handlers: evaluate and gate
description: Create evaluate and gate step handlers in internal/workflow/steps/. (1) evaluate.go: EvaluateHandler using CUE unification to validate previous wait output against expected schema. Finds most recent wait step output, compiles expected+actual as CUE values, checks subsumption. Detailed error on mismatch. (2) gate.go: GateHandler supporting two variants — human gate (writes gate_request tuples to tuplespace for each approver, polls for gate_response tuples, approve/reject, 30m default timeout, persistent gate state for crash recovery) and timer gate (pause for duration, crash-recovery-aware with remaining time calculation from original start). Tests: CUE evaluation matching/mismatch, human gate approve/reject/timeout, timer gate duration, gate crash recovery.

title: CLI daemon commands: pp daemon run/stop/status
description: Implement CLI subcommands for the pp binary: 'pp daemon run' (foreground daemon start), 'pp daemon stop' (send SIGTERM via PID file), 'pp daemon status' (check if daemon is running via PID file + process check). Wire these into cmd/pp/main.go argument parsing. Must include tests for CLI argument parsing and command dispatch. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments and usage help text.

title: Daemon Maggie classes: DaemonMain, Config, event loop
description: Implement Maggie-side daemon classes: DaemonMain (entry point that replaces Main.start when running as daemon), Config (loads maggie.toml settings), and the event-driven main loop using Channel select to multiplex IPC requests, checkpoint timer ticks, and shutdown signals. Must include tests exercising the Maggie event loop and config loading. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments in Maggie class comments.

title: Phase 1 Epic: Tuplespace (BBS)
description: Implement the Linda-style tuplespace that serves as the coordination backbone for multi-agent communication. Covers Go persistence layer, Maggie domain objects, the 4 Linda operations with blocking semantics, garbage collection, and agent mailboxes.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: TupleStore (SQLite+FTS5), Tuple/Pattern/TupleCategory domain objects, TupleSpace with 4 Linda ops and blocking, TupleGC, AgentMailbox+CrossPollinator, 13 integration tests all passing

title: Phase 9 Research: Config, Repo Registry & Work Tracking
description: Research configuration and supporting systems. Cover: TOML config (global + per-repo), feature flags with precedence (env > repo > global > default), repository registry (repos.json equivalent), work tracker interface and beads integration, onboarding/setup validation, hub system for future multi-node. Reference imp-castle's internal/config/, internal/repo/, internal/worktracker/. Output: docs/research/phase-9-config-and-registry.md

title: Phase 0 Epic: Project Setup & Build Pipeline
description: Epic covering all Phase 0 implementation tasks for procyon-park. Sets up project structure, build system, and verifies Maggie primitives work in the procyon-park context.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete: directory structure, maggie.toml, Makefile, Main.mag, cmd/pp/main.go, smoke tests all passing

title: Create Main.mag entry point and ProcyonPark namespace skeleton
description:
Create the entry point and namespace skeleton:
- src/Main.mag with Main class, class method 'start' that prints a version banner and exits
- Set up ProcyonPark namespace declaration
- Create skeleton .mag files in each src/ subdirectory with namespace declarations:
  - src/bbs/TupleSpace.mag (ProcyonPark::Bbs namespace, empty class stub)
  - src/agent/Agent.mag (ProcyonPark::Agent namespace, empty class stub)
  - src/daemon/Daemon.mag (ProcyonPark::Daemon namespace, empty class stub)
  - src/config/Config.mag (ProcyonPark::Config namespace, empty class stub)
- Verify 'mag ./src/... -m Main.start' runs and prints the banner
close_reason: Created src/Main.mag with ProcyonPark namespace, Main.start and Main.version class methods

title: Repo + config + workflow CLI commands
description: Implement three command groups: internal/cli/repo.go — 'pp repo register <path> [--name <name>]', 'pp repo unregister <name>', 'pp repo list', 'pp repo status [<name>]'. internal/cli/config.go — 'pp config get <key>', 'pp config set <key> <value>', 'pp config list', 'pp config edit'. internal/cli/workflow.go — stub commands for 'pp workflow run/list/show/cancel/approve/reject' that return 'not yet implemented' with proper exit codes (these will be filled in Phase 7). Wire daemon-side handlers for repo and config methods. Must include tests for repo/config commands and workflow stubs. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments and usage help.

title: Phase 1 Research: Tuplespace (BBS) in Maggie
description: Research reimplementing the Linda-style tuplespace in Maggie. Cover: Tuple representation as Maggie objects, the 4 Linda ops (out/in/rd/scan), pattern matching design, SQLite persistence via Go interop, blocking semantics using Maggie channels/processes, tuple categories (fact/convention/task/claim/obstacle/need/artifact/event/notification), lifecycle classes (furniture/session/ephemeral), TTL and GC, mail-as-tuple concept. Reference imp-castle's internal/bbs/ package. Output: docs/research/phase-1-tuplespace.md

title: Convenience commands: prime, status, version, init, completion, ping
description: Implement top-level convenience commands: internal/cli/prime.go — 'pp prime' outputs role-specific instructions using PP_AGENT_ROLE env var. internal/cli/status.go — 'pp status' combines daemon status + agent list overview. internal/cli/version.go — 'pp version' shows binary version. internal/cli/init_cmd.go — 'pp init' first-time setup (create ~/.procyon-park/, initialize config). internal/cli/completion.go — 'pp completion <shell>' generates bash/zsh/fish completions via Cobra. internal/cli/ping.go — 'pp ping' health check via system.ping IPC. Also add 'pp list' as alias for 'pp agent list' (imp-castle compat). Must include tests for each command. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments and usage help.

title: Tmux session primitives: Go package for session lifecycle
description: Implement internal/tmux package with Go functions: CreateSession(name, workdir, env) using tmux new-session with -e flags for env injection, KillSession(name), SessionExists(name) for liveness probes, SendKeys(session, keys) with Enter+delay, SendKeysLiteral without Enter, CapturePane(session, lines) for scrollback capture, ListSessions(). Session naming convention: pp-{repoName}-{agentName}. Must include unit tests for session creation/kill/existence checks (use short-lived tmux sessions in tests). All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments on all exported types and functions.

title: JSON-RPC dispatch: method routing + BBS bridge
description: Implement JSON-RPC method dispatch that routes incoming requests to handler functions. Implement the BBS bridge methods (tuple.write, tuple.read, tuple.take, tuple.scan) that translate JSON-RPC calls into VMWorker requests against the tuplespace. Return proper JSON-RPC error codes for unknown methods, invalid params, and internal errors. Must include tests for method routing, BBS bridge operations, and error handling. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments on all exported types and functions.

title: Template engine + embedded role templates
description: Create internal/prime/ package with Go text/template engine. Define TemplateData struct with typed fields (Role, AgentName, Repo, TaskID, Branch, Worktree, EnvPrefix string). Embed role templates via //go:embed templates/*.txt for: imp.txt (autonomous BBS worker — stigmergic coordination, atomic claiming, trail-leaving, completion protocol), king.txt (coordinator — creates conditions for work, spawns agents, reviews), reviewer.txt (code review specialist), merge-handler.txt (merge conflict resolver). Each template follows structure: identity statement → env vars → responsibilities → workflow → completion protocol → BBS communication → CLI reference. Implement LoadTemplate(role) with fallback chain. Implement ListRoles() and RenderTemplate(role, data). Must include tests for template loading, rendering with all data fields, and listing roles. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Respawn + prune: crash recovery and cleanup
description: Implement respawn sequence: validate agent not active → preserveWork (commit WIP + push branch) → cleanup for respawn (kill session, release name, remove worktree, preserve branch, unregister) → spawn new agent from preserved branch → send continuation message. Implement prune: git worktree prune all repos → find stopped/dead agents → verify tmux for active agents → remove worktrees → delete branches only if older than 24h (configurable --branch-age) → delete mailboxes → unregister → clean orphaned worktrees/mailboxes. Wire as daemon IPC methods (agent.respawn, agent.prune). Must include tests for respawn with work preservation, branch retention policy, orphan cleanup. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: BBS CLI commands: pp bbs out/in/rd/scan/pulse/seed-available
description: Implement internal/cli/bbs.go with all BBS commands: 'pp bbs out <category> <scope> <identity> [payload-json]', 'pp bbs in <category> [scope] [identity] --timeout <duration>' (blocking, exit code 4 on timeout), 'pp bbs rd' (non-destructive read), 'pp bbs scan [category] [scope] [identity]' (wildcard ? support), 'pp bbs pulse --agent-id <name>' (notification piggybacking to stderr), 'pp bbs seed-available <scope>'. Positional args match tuple structure. JSON payload as last positional arg. Wire daemon-side dispatch for all methods. Must include tests for each command, timeout/exit-code behavior, and wildcard matching. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments and usage help.

title: Phase 5 Research: Priming & Agent Instructions
description: Research the agent priming system. Cover: role-based instruction templates (king/imp/reviewer/merge-handler), how to store and render templates in Maggie, BBS-aware instruction transforms (replacing mail references with tuplespace commands), custom instruction overrides, how agents learn the CLI and tuplespace vocabulary. Reference imp-castle's internal/prime/ and templates/. Output: docs/research/phase-5-priming.md

title: Phase 5 Epic: Priming & Agent Instructions
description: Implement the priming system that gives agents identity, responsibilities, and BBS communication vocabulary at spawn time. Go text/template engine with typed data, embedded role templates (imp, king, reviewer, merge-handler), BBS protocol addendum, tuplespace context pre-reading, user-customizable overrides, middleware pipeline, and daemon-side handler wired to pp prime.

title: Tuple and Pattern Maggie classes
description:
Create the core domain objects in Maggie:

src/bbs/Tuple.mag:
- Tuple subclass of Object with instanceVars: id category scope identity instance payload lifecycle taskId agentId createdAt updatedAt ttlSeconds
- Factory class methods: category:scope:identity:payload:, fact:identity:content:, claim:identity:agent:status:, event:identity:payload:
- Instance methods: isFurniture, isSession, isEphemeral, assertConsumable
- Accessors for all fields, printString
- fromRow: class method to construct from a Store query result Dictionary

src/bbs/Pattern.mag:
- Pattern subclass of Object with instanceVars: category scope identity instance payloadSearch
- Factory class methods: any, category:scope:, category:scope:identity:, withPayloadSearch:
- matches: aTuple method for in-memory matching (nil fields = wildcard)
- toSQLWhere method that builds WHERE clause params for Store queries
- Accessors, printString

src/bbs/TupleCategory.mag:
- Standard category symbols with default lifecycles (fact->furniture, convention->furniture, notification->ephemeral, rest->session)
- Class methods for each standard category: fact, convention, claim, available, obstacle, need, artifact, event, notification, conventionProposal
close_reason: Implemented Tuple, Pattern, and TupleCategory Maggie classes in src/bbs/

title: Analytics CLI commands + daemon bridge
description: Add analytics and GC commands to CLI. (1) 'pp analytics performance [--repo] [--scope]' — agent performance stats. (2) 'pp analytics obstacles [--repo] [--min-count]' — obstacle clusters. (3) 'pp analytics conventions [--repo]' — convention effectiveness. (4) 'pp analytics knowledge [--repo]' — knowledge flow. (5) 'pp analytics signatures [--repo]' — workflow signatures. (6) 'pp gc run [--repo]' — trigger manual GC cycle. (7) 'pp gc status' — show last GC run stats. (8) 'pp feedback run [--repo]' — trigger manual feedback loop. (9) 'pp synthesis run --task <id>' — manual synthesis for a task. Daemon bridge: register analytics.*, gc.*, feedback.* RPC methods. Output: text tables + JSON for all commands. Tests: CLI arg parsing, output formatting.
close_reason: Analytics CLI commands + daemon bridge implemented with tests. All analytics tests passing.

title: [epic] Phase 7: Analytics & Storage Tiers
description: Analytics and tiered storage system: warm tier (Parquet via DuckDB), GC policies (TTL, stale claims, convention promotion, task archival, escalation), 6 DuckDB analytical queries, feedback loop, LLM-powered synthesis, cross-pollination, and CLI commands.
close_reason: Phase 7 complete: analytics CLI, daemon bridge, and comprehensive integration tests all merged and passing.

title: TupleStore Go primitive: SQLite CRUD with FTS5
description:
Implement the Go-side TupleStore as a Maggie primitive (vm/tuplestore_primitives.go). This is the persistence backbone:

- Register TupleStore class in Maggie VM with primitives for:
  - open:/openMemory class methods (creates SQLite DB, runs schema migrations)
  - insert: (category, scope, identity, instance, payload JSON, lifecycle, taskId, agentId, ttl) -> row ID
  - findOne: (pattern fields, nullable wildcards) -> Dictionary or nil
  - findAndDelete: (pattern fields) -> Dictionary or nil (atomic read+delete)
  - findAll: (pattern fields) -> Array of Dictionaries
  - delete: (id) -> boolean
  - deleteByPattern: (pattern fields) -> count
  - count: (pattern fields) -> integer
- Schema: v2 schema from research doc (tuples table, indices, FTS5 virtual table, triggers)
- WAL mode, busy_timeout=5000, synchronous=NORMAL
- FTS5 payload search when payloadSearch field is non-nil
- Prepared statement caching for hot queries
- Put in procyon-park Go code (not in maggie repo) as a custom primitive registration
- File: internal/tuplestore/store.go for the Go impl, internal/tuplestore/primitives.go for VM registration
close_reason: Implemented TupleStore with SQLite CRUD, FTS5, schema migrations, prepared statement cache, and VM primitives registration. All 11 tests pass.

title: Git worktree/branch primitives: Go package for isolation
description: Implement internal/git package with Go functions: CreateWorktree(ctx, repoPath, worktreePath, branchName, baseBranch), RemoveWorktree(ctx, repoPath, worktreePath) with force+fallback, IsValidWorktree(dirPath), PruneWorktrees(ctx, repoPath), GenerateBranchName(ctx, repoPath, agentName, taskID) producing agent/{name}/{taskID} with dedup suffix, MergeBranch(ctx, repoPath, source, target) with --no-ff, PushBranch(ctx, worktreePath, branchName), HasUncommittedChanges(ctx, worktreePath), CommitAll(ctx, worktreePath, message), IsProtectedBranch() with case-insensitive checks, ListOrphanedWorktrees(baseDir). Worktree path convention: ~/.procyon-park/worktrees/{repo}/{name}/. Must include tests for branch generation, worktree creation/removal, protected branch checks, orphan detection. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 1 integration tests
description:
Integration tests for the full tuplespace stack:

test/bbs_test.go (Go tests using Maggie VM):
- TestTupleStoreBasicCRUD: open in-memory, insert, findOne, findAll, delete
- TestTupleStoreFTS5: insert with JSON payload, search via payloadSearch
- TestTupleStoreMigrations: open, verify schema version
- TestTupleOutIn: out a tuple, in with matching pattern, verify consumed
- TestTupleOutRd: out a tuple, rd with pattern, verify NOT consumed
- TestTupleScan: out multiple, scan with partial pattern
- TestFurnitureProtection: out furniture tuple, verify in() returns Failure
- TestBlockingIn: fork out after delay, verify in() blocks and receives
- TestBlockingTimeout: in() with short timeout, verify Failure
- TestGCExpiredEphemeral: create expired ephemeral, run GC, verify deleted
- TestGCStaleClaims: create old claim + task_done event, run GC, verify cleaned
- TestMailboxSendDrain: send notifications, drain, verify exactly-once
- TestConventionPromotion: two agents propose same convention, run GC, verify furniture

All tests should use in-memory SQLite for speed.
close_reason: All 13 Phase 1 BBS integration tests pass: CRUD, FTS5, migrations, out/in/rd/scan, furniture protection, blocking, timeouts, GC (ephemeral + stale claims), mailbox send/drain, convention promotion

title: Phase 7 Research: Analytics & Storage Tiers
description: Research the analytics and tiered storage system. Cover: DuckDB warm tier for archived tuples, Parquet export from SQLite, GC policies (TTL expiry, stale claim detection, convention promotion, escalation detection), analytics queries (agent performance, obstacle clustering, convention effectiveness), cold tier archival to S3, synthesis (LLM-powered knowledge extraction). Reference imp-castle's internal/bbs/gc.go, analytics.go, synthesis.go. Output: docs/research/phase-7-analytics.md
close_reason: Research complete: docs/research/phase-7-analytics.md covers all requested topics

title: Daemon prime handler + CLI wiring
description: Wire the prime pipeline to the daemon's IPC dispatch. Implement system.prime handler that receives {role, agent_name, repo, task_id, branch, worktree} params, runs the prime pipeline (load template → override → addendum → context → render), and returns the rendered instructions as a string result. Update the existing pp prime CLI command (from Phase 4) to send proper params from PP_* env vars. Ensure pp prime works both with daemon running (IPC call) and has graceful error when daemon is down. Must include tests for the daemon handler and CLI round-trip. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Procyon Park: Cleanroom Imp-Castle Reimplementation Research
description: Epic covering all research phases for reimplementing imp-castle in Maggie (Smalltalk-in-Go). Each phase produces a research document in docs/research/ covering architecture, design decisions, Maggie idioms, and implementation plan.
close_reason: All 10 research phases complete, contradictions resolved, docs harmonized

title: AgentMailbox and CrossPollinator
description:
Implement agent-scoped notification system:

src/bbs/AgentMailbox.mag:
- AgentMailbox subclass of Object with instanceVars: space agentId
- Factory: for:in: (agentId, TupleSpace)
- send: aMessage - writes notification tuple (category: notification, scope: agentId, lifecycle: ephemeral, ttl: 300)
- drain - atomic scan+delete of all notifications for this agent, returns Array
- count - number of pending notifications

src/bbs/CrossPollinator.mag:
- CrossPollinator subclass of Object with instanceVars: space rateLimiter
- Factory: on: aTupleSpace
- checkCrossReferences: aTuple - scan payload for scope references, generate notifications
- extractScopeReferences: aPayload - parse payload Dictionary for scope-like strings
- Rate limiting: max 1 notification per (source-agent, target-scope) per 5 minutes
- Hooked into TupleSpace out: as a post-insert callback

title: Phase 9: Configuration, Repository Registry & Work Tracking
description: Supporting systems: TOML config (global + per-repo layered precedence with env overrides), feature flags, repository registry (JSON with staleness detection), expanded work tracker interface (beads integration via bd CLI), onboarding (pp init, pp repo add), setup validation (pp doctor), node identity, and directory layout.
close_reason: Phase 9 complete: 26 files, 5259 lines. TOML config, repo registry, work tracker, onboarding, pp doctor, integration tests.

title: TOML config: global + per-repo layering
description: Two-layer TOML config system. Global at ~/.procyon-park/config.toml, per-repo at <repo>/.procyon-park/config.toml. Config struct with sections: Agent (command, max_concurrent), Daemon (poll_interval, http_port), Telemetry (enabled, endpoint), Hub (enabled, listen_addr, peers, auto_discover), Features (bbs_enabled, workflows_enabled, telemetry_otel, hub_discovery). Leaf-level merge: per-repo overrides individual fields, not whole sections. Env override convention: PP_ prefix + TOML path uppercased with _ separators. defaultConfig() compiled defaults. sync.Once singleton with per-repo cache (map[string]*Config). Validation at load time: reject unknown keys, invalid values. BurntSushi/toml parser. Tests: loading, merging, env overrides, validation, defaults, caching.
close_reason: Two-layer TOML config with leaf-level merge, env overrides, validation, caching. 12 tests passing.

title: Repository registry
description: JSON-based repository registry at ~/.procyon-park/repos.json. Repo struct: Name, Path, AddedAt, HasBeads, MainBranch, BBSScope (defaults to Name), HubNodeID. Three-step resolution: --repo flag (by name then path) → cwd git detection → error with pp repo add prompt. Path resolution: absolute → symlink eval → worktree-to-main-repo via git rev-parse --git-common-dir. Name collision: append @parent-dir. Main branch detection: git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD, default main. Staleness detection on repo list: path exists? git repo? main branch changed? Advisory warnings, not auto-removal. File locking on writes (withFileLock pattern). Atomic writes via temp+rename. Tests: CRUD, resolution, symlinks, collisions, staleness, locking, atomic writes.
close_reason: JSON repo registry with resolution, staleness detection, atomic writes, file locking. All tests passing.

title: Work tracker interface + beads integration
description: Expanded WorkTracker interface with 8+ methods. Core CRUD: GetTask(id), CreateTask(CreateTaskOpts), CloseTask(id), UpdateTask(id, UpdateTaskOpts). Queries: ListReady(), ListByStatus(status), ListByParent(epicID). Dependencies: AddDependency(taskID, dependsOnID). CreateTaskOpts: Title, Description, TaskType, Priority, Labels, Parent. UpdateTaskOpts: pointer fields for Status, Assignee, Notes, Title, Description. Beads implementation: shell out to bd CLI with --json output parsing for all commands. Tracker selection at startup: hasBeadsDir → beads.New(), else noop.New(). Global default via SetDefault/Default. Mock tracker for tests. Tests: interface compliance, beads integration, noop tracker, mock tracker.
close_reason: WorkTracker interface with beads/noop/mock implementations, VM primitives, daemon wiring. 12 tests passing.

title: Onboarding: pp init + pp repo add
description: pp init (system setup): Create ~/.procyon-park/ directory structure, write default config.toml with commented examples, generate Ed25519 keypair → UUID v5 node ID (stored at identity/node.json + node.key), initialize BBS tuplespace storage, print next steps. Idempotent — never overwrites existing config or identity. pp repo add (repository onboarding): Resolve path → absolute → symlink-eval → main-repo-root, validate git repo, detect .beads/ and main branch, register in repos.json, create <repo>/.procyon-park/ if needed. No interactive prompts. Tests: init idempotency, directory creation, identity generation, repo add resolution, duplicate handling.
close_reason: pp init (idempotent setup, identity gen, BBS init) + pp repo add (resolution, detection). 9 tests passing.

title: pp doctor: setup validation command
description: Diagnostic command checking system health with green checkmarks / red X marks + actionable messages. Checks: ~/.procyon-park/ exists with correct structure, config.toml parses without errors, node identity exists and valid, BBS tuplespace accessible, git available at sufficient version, bd CLI available (if beads repos exist), tmux available, registered repos still exist and are git repos, daemon socket reachable (if expected running), no orphaned worktrees, no stale agent registrations. Output: text (colored checkmarks) and JSON format. Tests: each check in isolation, overall output formatting, failure cases with actionable messages.
close_reason: pp doctor with 11 health checks, text+JSON output, actionable messages. Tests passing.

title: Phase 9 integration tests
description: End-to-end integration tests for config, registry, and work tracking. (1) Config roundtrip: write global + per-repo TOML, load with LoadConfig, verify merge precedence. (2) Env override: set PP_* env vars, verify they win over file config. (3) Feature flags: toggle features via config and env, verify feature checks. (4) Registry lifecycle: pp repo add → list → staleness → remove. (5) Work tracker roundtrip: create task via tracker, query back, update, close. (6) Onboarding flow: pp init → pp repo add → pp doctor (all green). (7) Idempotency: run pp init twice, verify no corruption. (8) Error paths: invalid config, missing repo, broken identity.
close_reason: 14 integration tests passing: config roundtrip, env overrides, feature flags, registry lifecycle, work tracker, onboarding flow, idempotency, error paths.

title: Create Makefile and build pipeline
description:
Create the Makefile for procyon-park build orchestration:
- 'make pp' target: builds the pp binary (single binary with embedded Maggie image)
- 'make test' target: runs Maggie tests
- 'make clean' target: removes build artifacts
- 'make install' target: copies pp to ~/go/bin and codesigns (follow imp-castle pattern)
- The build should: compile .mag sources into image, embed image in Go binary, produce single 'pp' executable
- Reference Maggie's own build system (cmd/bootstrap, go:embed pattern) for the approach
- Verify the binary builds and runs successfully
close_reason: Created Makefile with image, pp, test, clean targets

title: Phase 3 Epic: Agent Lifecycle & Identity
description: Implement the full agent lifecycle system: identity, spawn/dismiss/respawn flows, tmux session management, git worktree isolation, name generation, registry persistence via tuplespace, liveness detection, crash recovery, and CLI commands. All agent lifecycle operations go through the daemon via IPC. Agent state lives in the tuplespace, not JSON files.

title: Create pp CLI entry point in Go (cmd/pp/main.go)
description:
Create the Go entry point for the pp binary:
- cmd/pp/main.go that embeds the Maggie image and boots the VM
- Use go:embed for the image file
- Register all native primitives (the 8 we built: exec, sockets, json, sqlite, toml, duckdb, cue, checkpointing)
- Parse CLI args: 'pp daemon run', 'pp daemon stop', 'pp daemon status' as initial subcommands
- For now, just dispatch to Maggie VM evaluation (call Main.start or a CLI dispatcher method)
- Use cobra for CLI framework (consistent with imp-castle)
- Verify 'go build -o pp ./cmd/pp' produces a working binary
close_reason: Created cmd/pp/main.go with embedded image loading, ProcyonPark::Main.start entry point, --version and --verbose flags. go vet passes.

title: Migrate daemon + agent commands to Cobra
description: Migrate existing cmd/pp/daemon.go and cmd/pp/agent.go commands to Cobra under internal/cli/daemon.go and internal/cli/agent.go. Add missing commands: 'pp daemon restart', 'pp agent show <name>', 'pp agent respawn <name>', 'pp agent attach <name>' (tmux attach), 'pp agent logs <name> --lines N' (capture pane), 'pp agent stuck <name>'. All commands use the IPC client to talk to daemon. Wire daemon-side handlers for new methods. Use output formatters for response display. Must include tests for all new and migrated commands. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments and usage help text.

title: BBS protocol addendum + tuplespace context builder
description: Create internal/prime/addendum.go with AgentPromptAddendum(scope, taskID) that generates a static BBS protocol reference block: tuplespace categories/scopes/identities, atomic claiming protocol (consume available → write claim), trail-leaving (obstacle, need, artifact tuples), completion protocol (mandatory task_done event), pulse cadence, notification piggybacking, full pp bbs CLI reference. Parameterized with scope and taskID. Create internal/prime/context.go with BuildAgentContext(store, scope, taskID) that pre-reads tuplespace state: furniture tuples (conventions, facts), active session tuples (claims, obstacles, needs from last hour), task-specific escalations. Cap at 10 per category with omission notice. Format payloads as key=value summaries. Must include tests for addendum generation and context building with various tuplespace states. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 6 integration tests: workflow engine end-to-end
description: Create comprehensive integration tests for the workflow engine. (1) Full workflow execution: define a CUE workflow with spawn→wait→evaluate→dismiss, run through executor with mock spawn/kill, verify instance state transitions and step results. (2) Parameterized workflow: test _input variable resolution with different param values. (3) Aspect expansion integration: workflow with aspects that inject gate before spawn, verify expanded step sequence. (4) Human gate flow: run workflow with human gate, simulate approve/reject via tuplespace tuples, verify gate state persistence. (5) Timer gate: run workflow with short timer gate, verify completion. (6) Crash recovery: create running instance, simulate daemon restart, verify resume with live agent. (7) Cancellation: start workflow, cancel mid-execution, verify cleanup. (8) Timeout handling: step-level and handler-level timeouts. (9) CLI integration: workflow commands against mock daemon.

title: Phase 2 Research: Daemon Architecture
description: Research the Maggie daemon design. Cover: long-running VM image as daemon, IPC server (Unix socket JSON-RPC vs gRPC Connect), image checkpointing and crash recovery strategy, polling loops vs event-driven design in Maggie, startup sequence, PID file management, graceful shutdown. How does the Maggie eval service map to daemon IPC? Reference imp-castle's internal/daemon/ package. Output: docs/research/phase-2-daemon.md

title: Workflow executor and crash recovery
description: Create internal/workflow/executor.go with the central Executor struct. (1) Executor configured via functional options (WithTelemetry, WithNotifyFunc, WithCancelKillFn, WithLivenessChecker, WithCompletionNotifier). (2) Run() method: parse workflow, validate params (reject unknown, apply defaults, check required), resolve workflow, create instance, launch executeLoop goroutine. (3) executeLoop(): sequential step dispatch with cancellation checks, step-level timeout via context.WithTimeout, handler lookup from registry, _ctx resolution via ResolveStepConfig, StepResult persistence, failure handling with notification. (4) Cancel(): cancel context + dismiss active agent. (5) RecoverOnStartup(): list running instances, check agent liveness, resume or fail. (6) Instance state machine: pending→running→completed/failed/cancelled. (7) Running instance tracking with cancellation map. Tests: full execution flow, param validation, step timeout, cancellation, crash recovery, failure notification.

title: Phase 3 integration tests: agent lifecycle end-to-end
description: Write integration tests that exercise the full agent lifecycle: spawn an agent (verify tmux session created, worktree exists, branch created, agent registered in tuplespace), check agent status/liveness, dismiss agent (verify merge happened, resources cleaned up), respawn a dead agent (verify work preserved, new session created from preserved branch), prune stale agents (verify cleanup). Test epic-aware branching (spawn under epic, verify feature branch). Test name pool exhaustion fallback. All existing tests (smoke + Phase 1 BBS + Phase 2 daemon) must continue to pass alongside new tests.

title: Step handlers: spawn, wait, dismiss
description: Create internal/workflow/steps/ package with three core agent lifecycle step handlers implementing StepHandler interface. (1) spawn.go: SpawnHandler with SpawnFn callback. Creates beads task via worktracker, calls spawn function, updates instance context (ActiveAgent, ActiveBranch, ActiveRepo, TaskID). Cleans up orphaned task on failure. (2) wait.go: WaitHandler polling tuplespace for task_done events matching active agent. 5s poll interval, configurable timeout (default 10m). Sets PreviousOutput on match. (3) dismiss.go: DismissHandler with KillFn callback. Resolves agent from step results, calls kill function, clears context. Merge conflicts non-fatal. Each handler: config struct, Execute method, output struct. Tests: mock spawn/kill functions, mock tuplespace, timeout behavior, error paths.

title: Phase 8 Research: Telemetry & OTEL Integration
description: Research telemetry system design. Cover: SDLC event categories (agent/task/mail/git/session/error/workflow), event storage in BadgerDB vs SQLite, OTEL receiver (gRPC trace/metric/log ingestion), query engine for historical analysis, Parquet export, how Maggie processes can emit telemetry natively. Reference imp-castle's internal/telemetry/ and internal/otel/. Output: docs/research/phase-8-telemetry.md

title: Workflow state persistence
description: Create internal/workflow/state.go with SQLite-backed workflow instance persistence. (1) workflow_instances table keyed by (repo_name, instance_id) storing JSON-serialized Instance data. (2) workflow_instances_idx table for status index keyed by (status, repo_name, instance_id) for filtered queries. (3) workflow_gate_state table for gate crash recovery (startedAt, state, promptSent). (4) CRUD operations: CreateInstance, UpdateInstance (with atomic index maintenance in transaction), GetInstance, ListInstances (with status/repo filters). (5) Gate state operations: SaveGateState, GetGateState, DeleteGateState. (6) Instance ID generation: wf-<random hex>. Tests: CRUD, status index queries, gate state persistence, concurrent updates.

title: Agent spawn flow: multi-step orchestration
description: Implement the spawn sequence as Maggie orchestration backed by Go primitives: validate role → allocate name → detect epic (base branch becomes feature/{epicID} if task has parent epic) → create branch (agent/{name}/{taskID}) → create worktree → create tmux session with env vars (PP_AGENT_ROLE, PP_AGENT_NAME, PP_REPO, PP_TASK) → launch agent command → wait for shell prompt → send priming instruction → register agent. Entry points: Spawn(role, taskID, baseBranch) and SpawnInRepo(role, taskID, repoName, repoRoot, baseBranch). Wire as daemon IPC method (agent.spawn). Must include tests for the full spawn sequence, epic-aware branching, and environment injection. All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Phase 7 integration tests: analytics end-to-end
description: Create comprehensive integration tests for the analytics system. (1) Full archival pipeline: create session tuples, trigger GC, verify Parquet files written, verify hot-tier cleanup. (2) Archive-then-delete safety: simulate crash mid-archive, verify no data loss. (3) GC policy tests: TTL expiry, stale claim cleanup, convention promotion at quorum. (4) Analytics roundtrip: write known tuples, archive to Parquet, run each of 6 analytics queries, verify results. (5) Feedback loop: run analytics, run feedback, verify furniture tuples written back to hot tier. (6) Convention lifecycle: propose→promote→prune if ineffective. (7) Escalation detection: systemic obstacles, unclaimed needs, repeated failures. (8) Synthesis mock: test pipeline with mocked LLM responses. (9) Cross-pollination: verify notifications + rate limiting.
close_reason: All integration tests passing: archival pipeline, GC policies, analytics roundtrip, feedback loop, convention lifecycle, escalation detection, synthesis mock, cross-pollination.

title: Agent dismiss flow + liveness detection
description: Implement dismiss sequence: kill tmux session → merge branch to target (feature branch if epic, else main) with --no-ff → release imp name → remove worktree → delete branch (only after successful merge) → unregister agent. Critical: if merge fails, abort entire dismiss (work preservation). Implement liveness detection: getActualStatus(agent) checks tmux.SessionExists() — if stored active but session gone, actual status is dead. Wire as daemon IPC methods (agent.dismiss, agent.status). Must include tests for dismiss sequence, merge-failure abort, liveness detection (active→dead transition). All existing tests must continue to pass. Add doc comments.

title: Create project directory structure and maggie.toml manifest
description:
Set up the procyon-park directory layout per the research doc:
- src/ with subdirs: bbs/, agent/, daemon/, cli/, config/, workflow/, telemetry/
- lib/ for shared Maggie libraries
- test/ for test files
- Create maggie.toml with project name 'procyon-park', namespace 'ProcyonPark', version '0.1.0', source dirs, entry point Main.start, image output config with include-source=true
- Ensure go.mod is correct (already exists but may need updates for maggie dependency)
- Do NOT set up gowrap in maggie.toml — all primitives are now native in Maggie
close_reason: Created directory structure (src/bbs,agent,daemon,cli,config,workflow,telemetry + lib, test, cmd/pp), maggie.toml manifest, updated go.mod with maggie dependency
